XSLmodels 0.2.0
  • Get started

  • Changelog

Kachergis 2012

Source: R/model-unfam_sampling.R
uncfam_sampling.Rd

Kachergis et al. 2012 uncertainty- and familiarity-biased associative model

Usage

uncfam_sampling(X, B, C, K = 1)

Arguments

X

Associative weight to distribute

B

Weighting of uncertainty vs. familiarity

C

Decay

K

Number of associations to update per word (constraint: B <= X)

Value

An object of class xslMod

Examples

mod <- uncfam_sampling(X = .1, C = 1, B = .98, K = 1)
xsl_run(mod, get_example_ambiguous_condition())
#> $fits
#> $fits[[1]]
#> $fits[[1]]$sims
#> $fits[[1]]$sims[[1]]
#> $perf
#>         1         2         3         4 
#> 0.6666667 0.1111111 0.1111111 0.1111111 
#> 
#> $matrix
#>      1    2    3    4
#> 1 0.06 0.01 0.01 0.01
#> 2 0.06 0.01 0.01 0.01
#> 3 0.01 0.01 0.01 0.06
#> 4 0.01 0.01 0.06 0.01
#> 
#> $traj
#> $traj[[1]]
#>      1    2 3 4
#> 1 0.06 0.01 0 0
#> 2 0.06 0.01 0 0
#> 3 0.00 0.00 0 0
#> 4 0.00 0.00 0 0
#> 
#> $traj[[2]]
#>      1    2    3    4
#> 1 0.06 0.01 0.01 0.01
#> 2 0.06 0.01 0.01 0.01
#> 3 0.01 0.01 0.01 0.06
#> 4 0.01 0.01 0.06 0.01
#> 
#> 
#> $sse
#> numeric(0)
#> 
#> attr(,"class")
#> [1] "xslFit" "list"  
#> 
#> $fits[[1]]$sims[[2]]
#> $perf
#>         1         2         3         4 
#> 0.1111111 0.1111111 0.6666667 0.6666667 
#> 
#> $matrix
#>      1    2    3    4
#> 1 0.01 0.06 0.01 0.01
#> 2 0.06 0.01 0.01 0.01
#> 3 0.01 0.01 0.06 0.01
#> 4 0.01 0.01 0.01 0.06
#>

#> 3 0.00 0.00 0 0
#> 4 0.00 0.00 0 0
